items.mistake: Calling fit() or optimizer steps during production inference
items.fix: Keep inference path forward-only with frozen θ; retrain offline on new labeled batches and deploy a new checkpoint.
items.why: Updating θ on live traffic without controlled labels causes unpredictable model behavior and data leakage from production inputs.
items.example: Wrong: clf.fit(live_features, guessed_labels) in API handler. Right: clf.predict(live_features) only; schedule nightly retrain on curated labels.

items.mistake: Using predict() on training data and reporting that as generalization
items.fix: Evaluate on held-out validation/test sets the model never trained on; use predict only for reporting metrics on those splits.
items.why: Predict on training x reuses memorized θ — metrics look inflated compared to true inference on unseen data.
items.example: Report accuracy on X_test after fit(X_train, y_train), not on X_train after the same fit().

items.mistake: Forgetting model.eval() and torch.no_grad() during inference in PyTorch
items.fix: Set eval mode and wrap inference in no_grad() to disable dropout behavior and gradient bookkeeping.
items.why: Training-mode layers and gradient tensors add noise, memory overhead, and slower inference.

items.fix: Serialize the exact preprocessing pipeline with the model; validate schema and transforms in both fit and predict paths.
items.why: Inference receives x in a different scale or column order than training — ŷ is wrong even with correct θ.
items.example: Training fit StandardScaler on log(price); serving API sends raw price without the same log + scale transform.
